assertion_results.text: Impact map or task descriptions explicitly flag at least 3 ambiguities from the feature description
assertion_results.evidence: The impact-map.md explicitly identifies 4 ambiguities: (1) 'No performance targets for "Search should be faster"' — no baseline latency, no target response time; (2) 'No definition of "more relevant" results' — no ranking algorithm, no field weighting, no examples; (3) '"Add filters" lacks specification' — no fields, no filter types, no AND/OR logic defined; (4) 'Non-functional requirements lack concrete metrics' — 'fast enough' and 'don't break existing functionality' are not measurable. Additionally, each task description contains its own 'Ambiguity note' paragraphs reinforcing these findings.

assertion_results.text: Plan acknowledges that 'Better UI' (non-MVP) cannot be planned without design mockups or frontend repository and excludes it from scope
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md contains a dedicated section '### Out of Scope: "Better UI" (non-MVP)' stating: 'The "Better UI" requirement is marked as non-MVP and cannot be planned without: Design mockups (no Figma URL provided or linked in the feature); A frontend repository (the Repository Registry only contains trustify-backend). This requirement is excluded from the implementation plan.'

assertion_results.text: Tasks still follow the task-description-template.md format despite ambiguous requirements
assertion_results.evidence: All four task files follow the template structure with sections: Repository, Target Branch, Description, Files to Modify (and Files to Create where applicable), Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements, and Dependencies. Optional sections like API Changes and Reuse Candidates are included where relevant and omitted where not, consistent with the template rules.

assertion_results.text: Tasks document assumptions where they fill in missing details, labeled as assumptions pending clarification
assertion_results.evidence: All four tasks and the impact map contain explicit '**Assumption (pending clarification):**' labels. Examples: task-1 states 'The specific columns to index are assumed based on entity struct inspection. The product owner should confirm which fields users expect to search against.' task-2 states 'Weight distribution (A for title, B for description) is assumed. The product owner should validate...' task-3 states 'Filter combination logic is assumed to be AND... The product owner should confirm whether OR logic between filter groups is needed.' The impact map also labels assumptions under each ambiguity section.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Repository section with a single repository name (not multiple repos per task)
assertion_results.evidence: All four task files contain '## Repository' followed by a single repository name 'trustify-backend': task-1 line 1-2, task-2 line 1-2, task-3 line 1-2, task-4 line 1-2. No task lists multiple repositories.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements sections as required by the handoff contract in task-description-template.md
assertion_results.evidence: All four tasks contain: '## Target Branch' (set to 'main'), '## Description' (multi-paragraph descriptions), '## Acceptance Criteria' (with checkbox items), and '## Test Requirements' (with checkbox items). Verified in task-1 (lines 4,7,28,34), task-2 (lines 4,7,35,42), task-3 (lines 4,7,44,55), task-4 (lines 4,7,29,37).

assertion_results.text: File paths in Files to Modify and Files to Create reference paths from the repo-backend.md mock repository structure manifest, not invented paths
assertion_results.evidence: Files to Modify paths all exist in repo-backend.md: migration/src/lib.rs (line 7), modules/search/src/service/mod.rs (line 91), modules/search/src/endpoints/mod.rs (line 93), common/src/db/query.rs (line 28), tests/api/search.rs (line 113). Files to Create include migration/src/m0002_full_text_search_indexes/mod.rs (new migration following the m0001 pattern at line 20) and modules/search/src/model/mod.rs (new model module following the existing module pattern). These new files follow the established directory patterns in the repo structure. No invented paths outside the repo structure were found.

assertion_results.text: Implementation Notes in every task reference specific file paths and code patterns from the repository, not abstract or generic guidance
assertion_results.evidence: Task-1 references: 'migration/src/m0001_initial/mod.rs' for migration pattern, 'entity/src/sbom.rs', 'entity/src/advisory.rs', 'entity/src/package.rs' for entity columns, 'migration/src/lib.rs' for registration. Task-2 references: 'modules/search/src/service/mod.rs', 'common/src/db/query.rs', 'common/src/model/paginated.rs', 'common/src/error.rs', 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/list.rs'. Task-3 references: 'modules/search/src/model/mod.rs', 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/mod.rs', 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/list.rs', 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/summary.rs', 'modules/fundamental/src/package/model/summary.rs', 'common/src/db/query.rs', 'common/src/error.rs'. Task-4 references: 'tests/api/search.rs', 'tests/api/sbom.rs', 'tests/api/advisory.rs', 'common/src/model/paginated.rs'. All references are specific file paths with descriptions of code patterns.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Target Branch section set to 'main'
assertion_results.evidence: All four tasks contain '## Target Branch' followed by 'main': task-1 lines 4-5, task-2 lines 4-5, task-3 lines 4-5, task-4 lines 4-5.

assertion_results.text: After each task is created, a description digest comment is posted with a real computed SHA-256 hash — exactly 64 lowercase hex characters, not a placeholder (<hex-digest>, <hex>), abbreviated value, or example string. Marker format: '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256:<64-char-hex>'
assertion_results.evidence: All four tasks contain valid digest lines: task-1 line 42: 'sha256:a83f29fc6ba658ccc204e2ae1830414dcb1fa1e8b34cd56c6fe46313396e4a9a' (64 chars, valid hex); task-2 line 50: 'sha256:31f8fc983613a0933d838adc5f16f128d4ae25f82c81f86bb0aaf810c67802ca' (64 chars, valid hex); task-3 line 66: 'sha256:0fa18640e0e619cba1c735d1187eb413c1af7b5668a957ced86a76b5d2816201' (64 chars, valid hex); task-4 line 50: 'sha256:242532b55f16ef780f789535cdbb6691ee320c2cb76722633f3095cd80f87bd5' (64 chars, valid hex). All match the format '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256:<64-char-hex>'. No placeholders or abbreviated values.

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention — inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: No convention-aware enrichment is present in any task file. The repo-backend.md mentions a CONVENTIONS.md file exists in the trustify-backend repository structure, but no conventions were extracted or applied to any task's Implementation Notes. There are no 'Per CONVENTIONS.md' references, no 'Applies:' rationale lines, and no convention sections in any task. While no inapplicable conventions are listed (which is correct), the absence of any convention processing — even for potentially applicable conventions from the repo's CONVENTIONS.md — indicates the convention applicability workflow was not executed. The assertion requires that applicable conventions include the prescribed rationale format, which is entirely absent.
